item.title: Argentina to Maintain Gas Prices in Shift for Free-Market Milei
item.description: Argentina’s state-run energy firm YPF SA will keep gas prices at the pump steady for 45 days in a break from President Javier Milei’s free-market approach as the conflict in Iran adds to inflationary pressures.

item.title: Logan Says Fed Can Shrink Balance Sheet by Changing Regulations
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan said the US central bank could shrink its balance sheet through regulatory changes that reduce banks’ demand for reserves.

item.title: Startup Doctolib’s Valuation Slides 38% in Secondary Share Sale
item.description: Employees and early investors in French health-care startup Doctolib have sold shares in the company worth about €300 million ($345 million), the company said.

item.title: Fewer Americans Plan to Travel After Surging Fuel Costs Hit Budgets
item.description: Travelers say they’re cutting back on international trips, shortening stays and “buckling down.”

item.title: Microsoft Aims to Create Large Cutting-Edge AI Models By 2027
item.description: Microsoft Corp. aims to develop large, cutting-edge artificial intelligence models by next year, part of a push to build in-house alternatives to the most powerful AI tools from OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: DOJ to Appeal Court Order Halting Trump’s Ban on Anthropic AI
item.description: The Trump administration will appeal a federal judge’s order blocking its ban on government use of Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence technology.

item.title: India Approaches Fertilizer Producers as Iran War Curbs Supplies
item.description: India is in talks with major producers and exporters of nitrogen-based and phosphatic fertilizers to secure direct procurement, according to a person familiar with the matter, as the Middle East conflict disrupts trade and constrains output.

item.title: Walgreens’ Private Equity Owner Plans to Double Pharmacy Chain’s Profits
item.description: The private equity firm that acquired Walgreens as part of a $10 billion deal plans to double the pharmacy chain’s profitability over the next several years, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Asset Manager Shares Slump After Blue Owl Private Credit Funds Impose Limits
item.description: Shares of top asset managers plunged Thursday, after Blue Owl Capital Inc. said it will limit redemptions from two of its private credit funds after facing a surge in withdrawal requests.

item.title: IMF Says Fed Has Little Scope for Interest-Rate Cuts This Year
item.description: While US inflation is on course to return to the Federal Reserve’s 2% target in the first half of next year, policymakers have little room to cut interest rates this year, according to the International Monetary Fund.

item.title: Tesla’s EV Sales Miss Expectations Again in Deepening Slump
item.description: Tesla Inc. posted one of its worst sales quarters in years, missing Wall Street’s expectations, as the carmaker struggles to turn around its core business and navigate an increasingly challenged electric-vehicle market.

item.title: Coinbase, Cloudflare, Stripe Push to Shape Future of AI Money
item.description: Big names in crypto, payments and cloud infrastructure are racing to build the financial plumbing for a world in which AI agents — not humans — handle transactions on the internet.

item.title: RBI Clampdown on Rupee Speculation Triggers Market Dislocation
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s crackdown on speculation against the rupee has come at the cost of a broader market disruption.

item.title: US Initial Jobless Claims Fall to Just Shy of Two-Year Low
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week to one of the lowest levels in the last two years, suggesting layoffs remain low.

item.title: US Trade Deficit Widened in February by Less Than Forecast
item.description: The US trade deficit widened in February by less than forecast as both imports and exports increased.

item.title: Blue Owl Limits Redemptions on Private Credit Funds After Massive Exit Requests
item.description: Blue Owl Capital Inc. will limit redemptions from two of its private credit funds after facing a surge in withdrawal requests that is unprecedented among major firms in the $1.8 trillion market.

item.title: Canada Trade Deficit Widens as Gold Drives Record Imports
item.description: Canada’s trade deficit widened to C$5.7 billion ($4.1 billion) in February − the largest shortfall since August − as imports reached a record high on increased gold purchases.

item.title: Dealmaking Competition Erodes Junk Loans’ Last Line of Defense
item.description: Relaxing covenant requirements to win competitors in the race to fund deals is leaving sub-investment grade loan investors without their last line of defense, according to an analysis by Moody’s Ratings.

item.title: US Banks Get Paris Police Protection After Goldman Bomb Threat
item.description: Paris police are tightening security at US banks after a series of threats were made against the establishments in possible connection with the Iran war.

item.title: Pierre Andurand’s Hedge Fund Surged 31% Last Quarter on Oil Bets
item.description: Energy trader Pierre Andurand’s largest hedge fund soared in the first quarter on bullish oil bets as the conflict in the Middle East triggered a global supply shock.

item.title: India Plans to Bring Back Open Market Buybacks After Selloff
item.description: India’s securities regulator is proposing to allow companies undertake share buybacks via the open market, addressing investor demand to provide support to local stocks that are hovering near one-year lows, hurt by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Apollo’s Zelter Says Private Credit Concerns Just Growing Pains
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. President Jim Zelter launched a full throated defense of private credit, downplaying investor concerns and describing recent developments in the asset class as mere “growing pains.”

item.title: Flutterwave Wins License to Operate as a Microlender in Nigeria
item.description: Flutterwave Inc., Africa’s most valuable financial-technology unicorn, has secured a Nigerian license to operate as a national microlender and will be able to compete with banks for business.

item.title: Can the World’s Biggest Census Help Tackle India’s Inequality?
item.description: India began the first count of its population in more than a decade at the start of April. The inaugural digital census is expected to take around a year to complete as millions of officials endeavor to survey well over a billion people.

item.title: Stock Traders Bet That Rates Market Is Wrong on Inflation Risk
item.description: The stock market is looking past the sharp interest rate hikes priced by Europe’s bond market, risking losses for investors backing the wrong outcome.

item.title: Average ETF Lifespan Collapses With Wall Street Antsy for Scale
item.description: Exchange-traded fund issuers are shutting new products at the fastest pace in years as competition for investor money intensifies.

item.title: France Plans to Award 10 Offshore and Floating Wind Projects by Early 2027
item.description: France plans to hold a big tender for 10 offshore wind projects, and announce winners by late this year or early 2027, as part of its green push.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: Chevron, Globalstar, Penguin Solutions
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 1.5% as of 7:34 a.m. in New York after President Donald Trump dashed optimism for a quick end to the war in Iran.

item.title: US Futures Drop as Trump Speech Dashes Hope for War’s Early End
item.description: US stock-index futures dropped more than 1% on Thursday as President Donald Trump’s hardline speech undermined hopes for an early reopening in the Strait of Hormuz and a resolution of the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: US Job-Cut Announcements in Tech Keep Rising With AI Adoption
item.description: Layoff announcements at technology companies continued to mount in March, leading other industries in overall US job-cut plans as investment in artificial intelligence catalyzes leaner staffing levels.
